track.title: Land Due Diligence Basics
track.description: A literacy route for checking land claims before paying deposits or signing documents.

course.title: Land Due Diligence Basics: Core route
course.description: Understand title checks, seller verification, public-land questions, and professional review before money moves.

lessons.title: Why land needs slow verification
lessons.summary: Land decisions are high-value, slow-liquidity decisions; pressure should trigger extra checks.
lessons.introduction: Land is emotionally powerful in Kenya, but it is also expensive, slow to sell, document-heavy, and vulnerable to pressure selling. A beginner should separate the dream of ownership from the evidence that the parcel, seller, and transaction are legitimate.
sections: Liquidity is low: selling land quickly can be difficult, especially outside active markets.
Verification is layered: title search, seller identity, boundaries, planning, rates, land rent, and public-land questions may all matter.
A deposit should come after documentation review, not before because a seller says another buyer is waiting.

scenario.title: Weekend viewing pressure
scenario.text: A buyer is shown plots on Saturday and told to send KES 50,000 before Monday. They pause, request parcel details, and plan an official search and professional review first.

lessons.mistake: Sending a deposit before verifying the parcel, seller, title status, and transaction documents.
lessons.action: Write a no-deposit checklist: official search, seller ID/KRA PIN, survey/boundary check, county questions, lawyer review, and payment trail.
lessons.takeaway: Land literacy begins with patience and evidence.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Use official land-service channels and qualified legal/survey professionals before committing money.

lessons.title: Official search and seller checks
lessons.summary: A title document is not enough; buyers should verify current records and seller authority.
lessons.introduction: A title shown on WhatsApp, email, or paper is only the start of verification. The learner should understand why current official records, identity checks, consent documents where applicable, and transaction trail matter before money leaves their account.
sections: An official search helps confirm registered details and whether restrictions, charges, or cautions appear in the record.
Seller authority matters: individual, spouse, company, estate, agent, or group seller situations need different evidence.
Payment records should match the agreement and parties; cash pressure makes later dispute resolution harder.

scenario.title: Agent-only sale
scenario.text: An agent asks for a deposit but cannot show written authority from the owner. The learner records this as a major verification gap.

lessons.mistake: Assuming an agent, broker, or relative can sell land without written authority and verified owner consent.
lessons.action: List the seller type and the documents needed to show they can sell the parcel.
lessons.takeaway: Verify both the land record and the person asking for money.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Confirm the applicable official-search route and legal requirements with official channels and licensed professionals.

lessons.title: Boundaries, county checks, and public land
lessons.summary: The parcel on the ground must match records, planning rules, access, and public-land restrictions.
lessons.introduction: A land buyer can see a fence, beacon, road, or map and still need verification. Boundary, planning, access, utility, rates, public-land, and dispute questions can affect whether a parcel is usable and transferable.
sections: A survey or boundary check helps confirm the parcel shown is the parcel in records.
County records, rates, planning, and access questions can affect cost and usability.
Public land, road reserves, riparian land, school land, or disputed land require heightened caution and professional review.

scenario.title: Nice plot, unclear access
scenario.text: A buyer likes a plot but cannot confirm legal road access. They pause because access can affect value, use, and resale.

lessons.mistake: Only checking the title number and ignoring the physical parcel, boundaries, county status, and access.
lessons.action: Before a deposit, record parcel number, official search status, boundary verification plan, county questions, and professional reviewer.
lessons.takeaway: A clean-looking viewing is not the same as complete due diligence.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Public-land and boundary questions should be verified with official records and qualified professionals.

lessons.title: Land reflection before deposit
lessons.summary: Use a journal prompt to document what is verified, what is missing, and who reviewed it.
lessons.introduction: A private decision journal protects the learner from excitement and pressure. Before a deposit, the user should write what is confirmed, what is unconfirmed, who checked it, and what would make them walk away.
sections: A journal entry should separate facts from promises made by a seller or agent.
Unverified items should stay visible until resolved by official documents or professional review.
The amount entered can stay as a range or hidden if the user does not want exact values in the app.

scenario.title: Private pause
scenario.text: A buyer records: official search pending, survey not done, seller authority unclear. They decide not to pay until these are complete.

lessons.mistake: Using the journal only after money is paid instead of before the decision.
lessons.action: Create a journal entry with three columns: verified, not verified, and next question.
lessons.takeaway: Writing the gap list can stop avoidable land mistakes.
lessons.source_note: Source note: This is an education prompt, not legal advice.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should happen before paying a land deposit?
options: Verify parcel, seller authority, and documents
Use only a photo of title
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Verify parcel, seller authority, and documents
quiz_questions.explanation: Land needs layered verification before money moves.

options: The shown plot must match records and be usable
quiz_questions.correct_answer: The shown plot must match records and be usable
quiz_questions.explanation: Physical and record checks both matter.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should a land journal entry capture?
quiz_questions.explanation: The journal helps keep evidence clear.

flashcards.back: It may take time to find a buyer, complete documents, and receive payment.
flashcards.example: A plot cannot usually be converted to cash like mobile savings.

flashcards.back: Evidence that the person selling can legally sell or represent the owner.
flashcards.example: Written authority for an agent or company resolution for a company sale.

flashcards.back: It helps connect money paid to the agreement and parties.
flashcards.example: Avoid cash pressure when a written trail is possible.

flashcards.back: Checking that the land shown on the ground matches the recorded parcel.
A missing or worrying item that should stop the transaction.
flashcards.example: Seller cannot show authority to sell.

resources.title: Land before deposit checklist
resources.body: A practical checklist for Kenyan land learners before paying deposits: verify the parcel, seller, official records, boundaries, county obligations, dispute questions, and professional review.
items: Run or request the applicable official search.
Use a lawyer for documents before deposit.
